notes: Morning flow was good — liked the yoga poses, added extra flow transitions. Accidentally loaded 70/side on bench first (math wrong), no spotter so backed off. Wants to consider machine alternatives for heavy bench to build confidence. Swapped incline DB for incline barbell since bench was right there. Push press is HARD. Cable lat raise machine didn't have 20 setting, jumped to 30 and it was fine? Spontaneously added straight-arm pulldown and pull-ups at end — had energy left despite some exercises being at max.
cardio: 12 min zone 2 run to gym + 12 min zone 2 run back
morningFlow: Did fb-w3-push-morning-flow. Liked the yoga poses, added extra flow movements. Wants more flowing transitions and straddled-leg stretches baked in for next time.

exercises.notes: Loaded 70/side by accident (math wrong). No spotter so felt sketchy — backed off to 55. Losing depth by set 2, super hard by set 3 (broke mid-set), stopped out at 6 on set 4. Wants to explore machine press alternatives for heavier work without a spotter.
SWAPPED to incline barbell since the bench was right there. 65 felt easy for 12. Jumped to 90 — got 10 then 8. Hard! Big jump from W2's 50lb DBs (different exercise though — barbell vs DB).
These are HARD. 'I really don't know what more fun means???' Dying by set 2, lost a rep on set 3. The leg drive helps but overhead is still brutal.
Got the actual pec deck this time (not Kinesis). 85x12 hard-ish, last two reps hard to hold. Bumped to 100 on set 3 — really hard but completed 10.
4 sets of 15x15. Form dropping at end of last two sets. Drop set settled to 8lb x12 to failure. Yes, form breaking = not clean reps. The drop set is the right call.
Same cable column as W2! Machine didn't have 20, jumped to 30x12x3 and wasn't too tired. Genuine strength jump from 15→30. Working weight is now 30.
50x12 not too hard. Bumped to 55 — hard by rep 10. Hands drifted together on last set which made it easier. (Yes, hands together shifts load — keep them apart for full tricep isolation.)

ADDED spontaneously — had energy and was already at cables. Actually really hard and felt like it fits. (Yes, this IS a pull/lat exercise — but it's fine here as a finisher. Lats aren't fatigued from push work so you're not compromising tomorrow.)
Added at end, alternated with other work. Way easier today than any other day — fresh back muscles on a push day. Don't read too much into it.

notes: KB Explore day. Loved the bent press — wants to do a full flow next week on easy weights for fluidity. Bent press at 25 is the right working weight (balance + tension challenge). Cossack squat easy at 45 (expected — bodyweight movement with light counterbalance). TGU at 25 is doable, left side still unstable but completed all reps. Windmills felt easy at 25 but hard at the same time — did extra reps. Clean & press at 50 is CLICKING — 3x6 both sides fluid nonstop. Catching the bell on the way up with slight knee bend = zero impact. Way more coordinated on right vs left but progressing. Rested more between C&P sets for clean form — worth it.
cardio: 12 min zone 2 run to gym + 12 min zone 2 run back

exercises.notes: NEW exercise — loved it! 20 felt easy, 25 doable but feel the balance and tension. Can screw up the balance so 25 is the right working weight. Wants to do full flow with bent press next week on easy weights for fluidity.
Easy at 45 but expected — this is more of a mobility/balance movement. Weight is counterbalance.
Bumped from 18→25, doable! Left side still unstable but completed all reps. Good weight — challenging enough to demand focus without compromising form.

sets: 25lb - extra reps beyond prescribed, same number of sets
exercises.notes: Felt easy at 25 but 'hard at the same time' — that's the flexibility/stability demand vs strength. Did more reps than asked. These are becoming a comfort movement.

HUGE improvement! 3x6 both sides fluid nonstop. First set was fluid nonstop — couldn't believe it. Getting good at catching the bell on the way up with slight knee bend = zero impact, then shooting straight up with whole body. Way more coordinated on right than left but progressing. Rested generously between sets for clean form. The technique is clicking!
No surprises at 50. Max KB weight — will start adding reps/sets to progress.
No surprises at 50. Max KB weight.
No surprises at 50. Max KB weight — will add reps to progress.

notes: Pull-ups 32 total (10/8/8/6) — beat W2's 27 but felt way harder than expected after two days of upper body. DB rows pyramided to 70x6 (PR!). Lat pulldown found a +10lb add-on plate, hit 150x8. Seated reverse fly modification (seated facing forward, bent over) engaged the right muscles way better. Preacher curl is humbling — 15lb/side is working weight. Grip/hands were fried by mid-session from all the pulling.
cardio: 12 min zone 2 run to gym + 12 min zone 2 run back

sets: BW x8 (last couple out of range)
exercises.notes: 32 total reps (beat W2's 27!). But felt way harder than yesterday/day before — cumulative fatigue from push + KB days. Last couple reps of set 3 not full ROM. Called set 4 at 6.

120 feels hard-ish at full ROM — good weight. Skips to 140 (no 130), very hard. Set 4 at 140 started leaning back too much — 120 is probably the honest working weight. Did 4 sets (realized after was supposed to be 3).
First set fine. Second set started struggling at rep 16. Third set hit a wall and only got 18. Harder than expected — fatigue accumulating.
Consistent at 42.5x12 for 3 sets, form starting to break on set 3. Added a 4th set, got 10 clean reps before form broke.
Pyramid continues to climb! 65x8 really hard — full body engagement. 70x6 is a new PR weight — form wasn't perfect but got it done. Not doing these statically — putting everything in gear.
Hands were DEAD from all the pulling — grip was the limiter, not lats. Found an extra 10lb add-on plate and tried 150 — significantly harder on same machine. Hands completely done by set 3.

MODIFICATION: seated facing forward, legs forward, bent over instead of standing. Way better muscle engagement — feels like it's hitting the right muscles. No lighter weights available so 25 it is. Surprisingly good weight in this position. Not sure if same weight would work standing.

exercises.equipment: preacher curl bench with EZ curl bars
exercises.notes: First time! 10/side x15 fine. Tried 25 total — impossible. 15/side: completely died at 10 reps. Third set got 6 good ones then died, dropped to 10/side and repped to failure at 10. Humbling exercise — way harder than cable curls at similar weight.

notes: KB Refine day. TGU bumped to 30 — hard, had to pause and reset on left side last rep. Overhead lunges at 50 are hard, legs ultra sore from prior days but loosened up after warming up. Squat front raise (Simba lift) pyramided 25→35→45 — child easy at 25, 45x8 with pause on top is the real challenge. Finisher complex done circuit-style (no rest between movements) at 45lb — absolutely brutal but completed 3 rounds! Observation: fatigue forces body to find more efficient movement paths. Alternated arms on OH lunges every 5 reps — need to check if that's intended.
cardio: 12 min zone 2 run to gym + 12 min zone 2 run back

exercises.notes: Light but deep. Went 10 per set instead of 5. Lower weight = deeper range, which is the point on refine day.
Feels easy at 18 — bumped to 5 reps per side instead of 3. Drilling the movement path. Ready for heavier next week.
Bumped from 25→30! Very hard. Had to pause on last rep left side and reset. Right side fine. Two sets of 3 each side. Good weight — demanding enough to force focus.
Definitely hard at 50. Left side not getting full range/struggle. Legs ultra sore even without weights — warmed up and stretched, felt better. Alternating arms every 5 reps. Three sets total.
Child easy at 25 compared to overhead KB work. 35 got much harder but did 10. 45x8 with a pause on top — this is where it gets real. The pause at top is the money rep.
5x3 at 18. Light and easy — comfort movement. Good active rest between harder exercises.

sets: 45lb circuit x3 rounds (no rest between movements)
exercises.notes: CIRCUIT STYLE — no rest between movements! Lighter weight (45 instead of 50). Thrusters bumped to 5 each instead of prescribed 4. Round 1: nonstop, super hard. Round 2: long rest then another full set — can't believe it. Round 3: had to take 10-second breaks before thrusters, holy shit so hard. Key insight: fatigue forces body to find more efficient movement paths. Says probably won't do circuit again but... can. This is a conditioning breakthrough.

notes: Legs day. Legs absolutely dead from KB conditioning yesterday — very sore going in. Squat needed extended warmup to loosen up (two sets at 55, stretching between sets). Once warm, 65/side felt great — hit 2x8 deep with good form. RDL solid at 65/side. Seated leg curl jumped to 105 on last set and still got 12. Leg press ROM is EXCELLENT — knee to chest even at 4 plates + 25s. Calf raises at 5 plates too easy, but felt the engagement all the way up through glutes which was new. Lunges at 45lb DBs were absolute death on cooked legs — barely survived the last set (split it with 30s rest). Great session despite soreness.
cardio: 12 min zone 2 run to gym + 12 min zone 2 run back

exercises.notes: Legs dead from yesterday. First 55x6 felt bleh, did a second 55x6 to warm up more. 65x6 struggled to reach full depth initially. Did more stretching/warmup — next 65x6 felt way better. Then got 2x8 deep quality reps. Needed extra warmup sets to loosen up sore legs. Maybe do more reps at 55 next time to get loose before jumping to 65.
8/10/10 — missed instructions on first set and could have done more. Last set hard but completed all with good form and not rushed. Consistent at 65.
90x12 twice, then bumped to 105 on last set — hard but still got 12 somehow. 105 is the new challenge weight. Big jump from W2's 50 → 90 → now 105.

Started at 3 plates to check full ROM — went basically knee to chest, felt good. Jumped to 4 plates — still really good range, don't feel like cutting ROM at all. Did two sets at 4 plates. Added 25s to each side on last set — still doable full range. ROM is excellent even at heavier loads.

45x5 plates/side = totally doable for 20. Second set felt it all the way up the back of the leg into the glutes — completely different engagement, not painful but felt the whole posterior chain working. 3x20 probably too easy — bump weight next time.
45lb DBs because all 40s were taken. Absolute death on cooked legs. First set brutal. Completed all 3 sets but had to split the last one in half with ~30s rest mid-set. Legs completely done.
